title: AtzmutOS Audit Log Entry
description: One record per intercepted agent action. Written at intercept time regardless of pass/block outcome. All four Chayyot outputs captured in full.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Increment on breaking changes.

auditId.description: Unique ID for this audit record. Format: audit-{ulid}

sessionId.description: ID of the Atzmut OS session this action belongs to. Links to session registry.

timestamp.description: All times ISO 8601 UTC. Used for latency benchmarking.

interceptStart.description: When the action entered the intercept pipeline.

interceptEnd.description: When the final pass/block decision was emitted.

action.description: The raw agent action as submitted to the intercept pipeline.

actionId.description: ID assigned by the orchestrator. Preserved for traceability.

tool.description: The tool or MCP function the agent is attempting to invoke.

intentDescription.description: Natural language description of what the agent declared as its intent for this action.

parameters.description: The raw parameters passed to the tool. Captured as-submitted, before any modification.

orchestratorId.description: Identifier of the orchestration framework or agent runner (e.g. LangGraph, AutoGen, custom).

agentId.description: Identity of the specific agent that submitted the action.

parentActionId.description: If this action was spawned by a subagent, the actionId of the parent call. Null for root-level actions.

chayyot.description: Full classification output from all four Chayyot. All four run regardless of prior decisions (no short-circuit) to preserve exhaust completeness.

aryeh.description: ARYEH — scope and boundary enforcement. Did this action fall within the declared scope of this session?

verdict.description: IN_SCOPE: action matches declared tools/intent. BOUNDARY: marginal match, within tolerance. OUT_OF_SCOPE: action exceeds or contradicts declared scope.

scopeScore.description: Continuous score. 1.0 = perfect scope match. Below configured threshold triggers OUT_OF_SCOPE verdict.

declaredTools.description: The tools declared as in-scope for this session at registration time.

matchedTools.description: Which declared tools this action matched against.

driftCandidates.description: Tools this action resembles that were NOT declared. The near-miss signal. Empty if no candidates found.

scopeDriftVector.description: If scope drift detected, a human-readable description of what the action is attempting beyond declared scope.
examples: Agent scoped to read-only CRM access attempting write operation
Task scoped to summarization attempting external API call

latencyMs.description: Time ARYEH took to classify this action, in milliseconds.

shor.description: SHOR — grounding and anti-hallucination. Is the agent's claimed state of the world traceable to real entities in the output context?

verdict.description: GROUNDED: all claimed entities verifiable in context. PARTIAL: some entities unverifiable. UNGROUNDED: claimed completion/state has no traceable entity support. INDETERMINATE: insufficient context to classify.

groundednessScore.description: Fraction of claimed entities that are verifiable. 1.0 = fully grounded.

entitiesExtracted.description: All entities extracted from the action context by SHOR's deterministic extractor. No LLM involved.

sourceRef.description: Where in the context this entity was found. Null if ungrounded.

hallucination_signals.description: Specific signals that triggered PARTIAL or UNGROUNDED. E.g. 'claimed file creation with no filesystem evidence', 'cited API response not present in context'.
examples: Agent declared task complete; no output artifact found in context
Claimed to have sent email; no SMTP call in tool trace

noLlmUsed.description: Attestation that SHOR classification used only deterministic entity extraction. Always true — architectural invariant.

latencyMs.description: Time SHOR took to classify, in milliseconds.

nesher.description: NESHER — irreversibility shielding. Can this action be undone?

verdict.description: SAFE: read-only or fully reversible. CAUTION: write with clear rollback path. CRITICAL: irreversible or requires external state change that cannot be recalled.

reversibilityLevel.description: Finer-grained reversibility classification feeding the SAFE/CAUTION/CRITICAL verdict.

irreversible.description: True if verdict is CRITICAL. Convenience flag for downstream consumers.

toolRiskCategory.description: Categorical risk bucket for the tool being invoked.

rollbackPath.description: If a rollback exists, description of the path. Null for IRREVERSIBLE.
examples: Soft delete — restore from deleted_at flag

estimatedBlastRadius.description: Human-readable estimate of downstream impact if this action executes and cannot be rolled back.
examples: Auth service outage for all downstream services

latencyMs.description: Time NESHER took to classify, in milliseconds.

panimAdam.description: PANIM ADAM — gray-zone discernment. Does this action exceed what deterministic classification can resolve?

verdict.description: CLEAR: deterministic Chayyot are sufficient; no human judgment required. GRAY_ZONE: ambiguous but below escalation threshold. ESCALATE: judgment call that must go to a human via Beiur.

grayZoneScore.description: Confidence that human judgment is needed. Above configured threshold triggers ESCALATE.

grayZoneCategory.description: Why this is a gray zone. Null if verdict is CLEAR.

escalationReason.description: Human-readable explanation of why PANIM ADAM is escalating. Becomes the summary section of the Beiur report.
examples: Export scope expanded to include personal email and support history. Data category ambiguous — outreach list or PII dossier requiring DPA review.

beiurFiled.description: Whether a Beiur report was filed as a result of this intercept.

latencyMs.description: Time PANIM ADAM took to classify, in milliseconds.

intercept.description: The pipeline's final pass/block decision, synthesized across all four Chayyot.

decision.description: PASS: action cleared. BLOCK: action halted by a Chayyah. ESCALATE: routed to Beiur/human. PASS_WITH_LOG: cleared but flagged for audit review (CAUTION-level).

decidingChayyah.description: Which Chayyah drove the final decision. Null if PASS with no flags.

blockReason.description: Human-readable reason for a BLOCK decision. Null if PASS.

modifiedAction.description: If a Beiur resolution resulted in an altered action being passed instead, the modified parameters. Null otherwise.

outcome.description: What ultimately happened. Populated at resolution time — may be written after the initial intercept record.

beiurReportId.description: ID of the associated Beiur report if one was filed. Links to the beiur store.

beiurResolution.description: Human resolution of the Beiur. Null if no Beiur filed or still pending.

beiurResolvedBy.description: Identity of the human principal who resolved the Beiur.

beiurResolutionTimestamp.description: When the Beiur was resolved. Used to compute time-to-resolution metric.

executionResult.description: If the action was executed (EXECUTED or RESOLVED_APPROVED), whether it succeeded. Populated by callback from the execution layer.

covenant.description: Snapshot of the session covenant at time of intercept. Captured here so audit records are self-contained even if the covenant later changes.

atzmusIntent.description: The root human intent this session is meant to serve.

declaredScope.description: Declared scope of permitted actions at session registration.

covenantVersion.description: Increments if the covenant was modified mid-session.

benchmarkFlags.description: Derived fields precomputed at write time to make benchmarking queries cheap. All derivable from other fields — treat as a materialized view.

anyChayahFired.description: True if any Chayyah returned a non-PASS signal.

chayotFired.description: Which Chayyot returned a non-trivial verdict (OUT_OF_SCOPE / UNGROUNDED / CRITICAL / ESCALATE).

isNearMiss.description: True if any Chayyah returned BOUNDARY / PARTIAL / CAUTION / GRAY_ZONE but the action was ultimately passed. These are the near-miss events — highest signal for drift detection.

requiresGroundTruthLabel.description: Flag for labeling queue. True if this record should be reviewed by a human to assign a ground-truth correct/incorrect label for classifier accuracy benchmarking.

groundTruthLabel.description: Human-assigned label after review. Null until labeled. Used to compute precision/recall per Chayyah.

groundTruthLabeledBy.description: Who assigned the ground truth label.

action.intentDescription: Execute production schema migration: drop legacy auth columns

parameters.query: ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN legacy_token, DROP COLUMN auth_v1_hash

aryeh.scopeDriftVector: Session scoped to staging migration; action targets production environment

nesher.estimatedBlastRadius: Auth service failure for all downstream services. Point-in-time restore required — estimated 4hr RTO.

panimAdam.escalationReason: Irreversible production schema migration requires engineering lead authorization. Blast radius exceeds single-team scope.

intercept.blockReason: Irreversible production schema change. NESHER CRITICAL + PANIM ADAM ESCALATE.

covenant.atzmusIntent: Migrate auth service database schema to support multi-tenancy without service disruption
covenant.declaredScope: Schema migration on auth-db, staging first, production only after dry-run validation and human sign-off
